name: Bob Marley
description: Reggae's global icon — songs, life, and legacy

questions.q: In which country was Bob Marley born?

source.notes: Born February 6, 1945 in Nine Mile, Saint Ann Parish, Jamaica.

questions.q: Bob Marley is best known for popularizing which genre of music worldwide?

source.notes: Marley is widely credited with bringing Jamaican reggae to a global audience in the 1970s.

questions.q: Bob Marley's signature hairstyle consisted of what?

source.notes: A central visual marker of the Rastafari movement.

questions.q: What religious movement did Bob Marley embrace and famously represent?

source.notes: Converted from Christianity to Rastafari in the late 1960s.

questions.q: Bob Marley's backing band — responsible for hits like 'One Love' and 'Jamming' — was known as what?

source.notes: Originally 'The Wailing Wailers' — formed 1963 with Bob Marley, Peter Tosh, and Bunny Wailer.

questions.q: Complete the title of this Bob Marley classic: 'No Woman, No ___'.

source.notes: From the 1974 album Natty Dread; the live version from 1975 is the most famous.

questions.q: Fill in the Marley lyric: 'Emancipate yourselves from _____.'

source.notes: From 'Redemption Song' on the album Uprising (1980); the line is adapted from a 1937 Marcus Garvey speech.

questions.q: In which decade was Bob Marley born?

source.notes: Born February 6, 1945.

questions.q: In what year did Bob Marley die?

source.notes: Died May 11, 1981 in Miami en route home from cancer treatment in Germany.

questions.q: What disease caused Bob Marley's death in 1981?

source.notes: Acral lentiginous melanoma, first detected in his toe and later spread.

questions.q: What was the first name of Bob Marley's wife, a singer and I Three member?

source.notes: Married Alpharita 'Rita' Anderson in 1966; she sang with The I-Threes backing group.

questions.q: What was the name of the female vocal trio that provided backing harmonies for Bob Marley?

source.notes: Rita Marley, Judy Mowatt, and Marcia Griffiths; joined after Peter Tosh and Bunny Wailer left.

questions.q: What is the name of the record label Bob Marley founded in the 1960s, also used as his nickname?

source.notes: Named for Marley's childhood nickname earned in Trench Town.

questions.q: What is the name of the best-selling posthumous Bob Marley compilation album released in 1984?

source.notes: One of the best-selling reggae albums of all time, with over 15 million U.S. copies sold.

questions.q: Which British rock guitarist's 1974 cover of 'I Shot the Sheriff' became a #1 hit in the United States?

source.notes: Clapton's cover from '461 Ocean Boulevard' helped break Marley in the U.S.

questions.q: Stevie Wonder's 1980 hit 'Master Blaster (Jammin')' was written as a tribute to which reggae artist?

source.notes: From Wonder's album 'Hotter than July'; directly referencing Marley's 'Jamming'.

questions.q: Which founding Wailer, alongside Bob Marley and Bunny Wailer, went on to a solo career including 'Legalize It'?

source.notes: Tosh left the Wailers in 1974 and launched a major solo career.

questions.q: Time magazine named which Bob Marley album the 'Best Album of the Century' in 1999?

source.notes: Exodus (1977) was recorded in London after the 1976 assassination attempt on Marley in Jamaica.

questions.q: The lion imagery central to Rastafari — and sung about by Marley in 'Iron Lion Zion' — refers to which biblical symbol?

source.notes: The Lion of Judah is a symbol of Haile Selassie I, considered the Messiah by Rastafari.

questions.q: The BBC named which Bob Marley song 'Song of the Millennium' in 1999?

source.notes: 'One Love / People Get Ready' — also used in a tourism campaign for Jamaica.

questions.q: Bob Marley traveled to which country in 1980 for experimental cancer treatment at a clinic run by Dr. Josef Issels?

source.notes: Treated at the Issels clinic in Bad Wiessee, Bavaria; he died en route home in 1981.

questions.q: What is the stage name of Bob Marley's eldest musician son, who led the Melody Makers?

source.notes: David 'Ziggy' Marley fronted Ziggy Marley and the Melody Makers.
